summary: Independent QA accepts the platform/resolution/localization matrix evidence. The branch adds observable Playwright matrix coverage and an explicit JSON fixture for supported Chromium/Windows desktop smoke, supported resolutions, supported locale states, and scoped unsupported rows. Edge, Firefox, and Windows DPI 150% are not represented as covered; they remain unresolved matrix rows for M7-UI-LOCALIZATION-EXIT-001 to carry or resolve.

tests_run.result: Clean tracked worktree on codex/m7-platform-resolution-localization-matrix before this QA handoff was written.
Changed files are process/task/message files plus tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts and tests/e2e/m7-platform-resolution-localization.matrix.json.
No whitespace or patch formatting errors.
No working tree whitespace or patch formatting errors.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for task and fixture

tests_run.result: No ready-task stdout in the current state.

tests_run.command: PowerShell allowed/forbidden path scanner over git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD
tests_run.result: No allowed-path violations and no forbidden-path hits.

tests_run.command: git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD -- forbidden path list
tests_run.result: No forbidden path output for sim-runner, content-tools, sim-core, sim-ai, protocol, save-format, tools, .github, frozen docs, PROJECT_STATUS, manifests, checksums, or validation reports.
Electron security preferences passed; preload exposes only the named monsoonDesktop bridge; allowed IPC channels are desktop:getRuntimeInfo and desktop:setFullscreen.
format:check, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundary checks passed.

Changed product-surface diff contains no Electron IPC, telemetry, account, cloud-save, network, or hidden-server implementation. Matches are limited to Node fs/path imports in the Playwright spec fixture loader.

tests_run.command: Read required files and related M7 handoffs
tests_run.result: Reviewed AGENTS.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, project/goal-mode-state.json, task JSON, writer handoff, tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts, matrix fixture, playwright.config.ts, route file, M7-UI-REGRESSION writer/QA/closed handoffs, M7-UI-LOCALIZATION-PLAN QA handoff, and related map/guidance/save-boundary QA handoffs.

acceptance_check.explicit_matrix_evidence: PASS: fixture records Chrome pass, Windows desktop smoke pass, four supported resolutions, three supported locale rows, and explicit unsupported rows for Edge, Firefox, and Windows DPI 150%. The fixture shape is asserted by the new Playwright test.
acceptance_check.surface_coverage: PASS: e2e suite and fixture cover tutorial, hints, encyclopedia, map, major command flows, save/load surface, and terminal/evidence views. QA reran full Chromium e2e and it passed 23/23.
acceptance_check.overflow_overlap: PASS: existing and extended e2e coverage checks no horizontal overflow across required desktop viewports and matrix scenarios; the full e2e rerun passed.
acceptance_check.edge_firefox_dpi_handling: PASS_WITH_FOLLOW_UP: Edge and Firefox are blocked-by-tooling because playwright.config.ts defines chromium only; Windows DPI 150% is a follow-up because no dedicated DPI harness exists. These rows are not fake pass evidence.
acceptance_check.m7_ui_localization_exit_dependency: PASS_WITH_RISK: M7-UI-LOCALIZATION-EXIT-001 depends on this task, so exit evidence must explicitly carry or resolve the Edge/Firefox/DPI rows before claiming full platform coverage.
acceptance_check.electron_renderer_boundary: PASS: no changed Electron/renderer implementation expands Node integration, arbitrary IPC, hidden server, telemetry, accounts, or cloud-save; pnpm desktop:security-check passed.
acceptance_check.locale_policy: PASS: expectedLang zh-CN is consistent with the existing i18n-platform normalization of zh-Hans/zh-Hans-HK to zh-CN while UI-visible supported locale labels include zh-Hans and en-US.

decisions.decision: Accept this task as a truthful platform/resolution/localization evidence matrix rather than full cross-browser enablement.
Edge, Firefox, and Windows DPI 150% are accepted only as unresolved blocked/follow-up matrix rows. They must not be described later as passed coverage unless tooling is added and evidence rerun.
M7-UI-LOCALIZATION-EXIT-001 may use this handoff as input, but it must explicitly decide whether the unsupported rows are acceptable scoped follow-ups, defects, or blockers for the exit gate.

Accept fixture expectedLang zh-CN for zh-Hans browser/system inputs because existing locale code normalizes Simplified Chinese variants to zh-CN and the supported-locale UI still exposes zh-Hans/en-US labels.

risks.summary: Edge and Firefox are not actually run in this workspace because Playwright config has only the chromium project.
risks.mitigation: Recorded as blocked-by-tooling rows. M7-UI-LOCALIZATION-EXIT-001 must not claim Edge/Firefox pass coverage without adding/running those projects or accepting a scoped follow-up.

risks.summary: Windows DPI 150% is not directly exercised by the current browser or desktop smoke tooling.
risks.mitigation: Recorded as a follow-up row. Exit validation must carry or resolve it.

risks.summary: Writer reported initial pnpm test:e2e and pnpm check failures before fixing fixture loading/formatting.
risks.mitigation: QA reran pnpm test:e2e and pnpm check successfully on the final branch state.

requested_action: Lead may accept/integrate M7-PLATFORM-RESOLUTION-LOCALIZATION-MATRIX-001. Preserve the Edge/Firefox/Windows DPI 150% unresolved-row risk for M7-UI-LOCALIZATION-EXIT-001 and do not mark those rows as passed coverage without new evidence.
